ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='### Step 2: Write the **Account** model. This is straightforward as it maps directly from the source `account` table. The `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` should be populated with the source `id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`). Defaults for `Website`, `BillingCity`, `BillingCountry`, `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `IsDeleted` will be set to `NULL` or sensible defaults.'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Account.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nSELECT\\n    id AS \\"Id\\",\\n    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(name), \'\'), \'Unknown\') AS \\"Name\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'platinum\' THEN \'Platinum\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'gold\' THEN \'Gold\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'silver\' THEN \'Silver\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'bronze\' THEN \'Bronze\'\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(region), \'\') AS \\"Region__c\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(industry), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Website\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n    id AS \\"Legacy_Customer_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }}\\n"}', tool_call_id='0WzFlAawz')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=12999, output_tokens=396), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 39, 344087, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 31, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-bf47aee0e2780841', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='write_file', content='wrote 781 bytes to models/Account.sql', tool_call_id='0WzFlAawz', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 39, 347845, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 39, 348296, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="### Step 3: Write the **Contact** model. This requires joining with the `account` table to resolve `AccountId`. The `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` is populated with `contact.id`. We also need to split `full_name` into `FirstName` and `LastName`. For `Role__c` and `Preferred_Language__c`, we'll default to `NULL` since the source does not provide this information."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Contact.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nSELECT\\n    c.id AS \\"Id\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN c.full_name IS NOT NULL THEN\\n            TRIM(SPLIT_PART(c.full_name, \' \', 1))\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"FirstName\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN c.full_name IS NOT NULL THEN\\n            TRIM(\\n                SUBSTRING(\\n                    c.full_name,\\n                    POSITION(\' \' IN c.full_name) + 1\\n                )\\n            )\\n        ELSE \'Unknown\'\\n    END AS \\"LastName\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(c.email), \'\') AS \\"Email\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Phone\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Title\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Role__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Preferred_Language__c\\",\\n    a.id AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n    c.id AS \\"Legacy_Contact_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'contact\') }} c\\nLEFT JOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }} a \\n    ON c.account_ref = a.id\\n"}', tool_call_id='GLxt6c9ly')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=13409, output_tokens=402), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 20, 996020, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 50, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-bd0882b8b6a6a4a0', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='write_file', content='wrote 919 bytes to models/Contact.sql', tool_call_id='GLxt6c9ly', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 20, 999791, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 21, 234, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="### Step 4: Write the **Opportunity** model. This requires joining with the `account` table to resolve `AccountId`. The join key is `opportunity.account_name` = `account.name` (after normalizing for typos and case). The `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` is populated with `opportunity.customer_number`. The `StageName` must map to the allowed enum values. `CloseDate` is not in the source, so we'll default it to `NULL`."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Opportunity.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nSELECT\\n    o.id AS \\"Id\\",\\n    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(o.name), \'\'), \'Unknown\') AS \\"Name\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'prospecting\' THEN \'Prospecting\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'qualification\' THEN \'Qualification\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'needs analysis\' THEN \'Needs Analysis\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'value proposition\' THEN \'Value Proposition\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'id. decision makers\' THEN \'Id. Decision Makers\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'perception analysis\' THEN \'Perception Analysis\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) IN (\'proposal\', \'proposal/price quote\') THEN \'Proposal/Price Quote\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) IN (\'negotiation\', \'negotiation/review\') THEN \'Negotiation/Review\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'closed won\' THEN \'Closed Won\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'closed lost\' THEN \'Closed Lost\'\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"StageName\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CloseDate\\",\\n    o.amount AS \\"Amount\\",\\n    \'EUR\' AS \\"CurrencyIsoCode\\",\\n    a.id AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n    o.customer_number AS \\"Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'opportunity\') }} o\\nLEFT JOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }} a \\n    ON TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(LOWER(o.account_name), \'[^a-z0-9\\\\s&\\\\.\\\\-]\', \'\', \'g\')) = \\n       TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(LOWER(a.name), \'[^a-z0-9\\\\s&\\\\.\\\\-]\', \'\', \'g\'))\\n"}', tool_call_id='Y6tmcihfR')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=13825, output_tokens=642), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 47, 464495, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 31, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-ab017a3f929d2032', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 29, 48, 286200, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now I have a clear picture of the relationships and data formats. Here's what I've deduced:

### Key Mappings:
1. **Account to Opportunity**:
   - `opportunity.customer_number` (e.g., `KD-1001`) does **not** directly match `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - However, `opportunity.account_name` matches `account.name` (with some typos like `GRb` vs `GbR`).
   - To map `opportunity` to `account`, we must join on **normalized `account_name` = `account.name`**.
   - The `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` should be populated with `opportunity.customer_number`.

2. **Contact to Account**:
   - `contact.account_ref` directly references `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - Some `account_ref` values are `NULL` or invalid (e.g., `ACC-9103`), so we must handle these with `LEFT JOIN`.
   - The `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` should be populated with `contact.id`.

3. **Project to Account/Opportunity**:
   - `project.client_id` directly references `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - `project.opportunity_ref` directly references `opportunity.id` (e.g., `OPP-00021`).
   - The `Legacy_Project_ID__c` should be populated with `project.id`.

4. **Asset to Account/Project**:
   - `asset.client` is **inconsistent**: sometimes it's an `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1005`), sometimes it's an `account.name` (e.g., `Gnatz AG`).
   - To resolve this, we must **first try to join on `account.id`**, and if that fails, join on `account.name`.
   - `asset.project` directly references `project.id`.
   - The `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` should be populated with `asset.id`.

5. **Enum Mappings**:
   - **Account Tier**: `Bronze`, `Silver`, `Gold`, `Platinum` → map directly to `Customer_Tier__c`.
   - **Opportunity Stage**: Map `stage` values to the allowed enum values in `StageName`.
   - **Project Status**: Map `status` values to `Project_Status__c` (e.g., `Completed`, `On Hold`, etc.).
   - **Contact Role**: Not directly available in source, so we'll default to `NULL` or a sensible default (e.g., `End User`).
   - **Preferred Language**: Not directly available in source, so we'll default to `NULL`.

6. **Dates**:
   - `warranty` and `go_live` are in `YYYY-MM-DD` format, so we can directly cast them to text.
   - For `CreatedDate` and `LastModifiedDate`, since they are not in the source, we'll use `NULL` or a default timestamp.

7. **Amounts**:
   - `opportunity.amount` is already a `double precision`, so we can directly use it.

### Transformation Plan:
- **Account**: Directly map from `fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.account`.
- **Contact**: Join with `account` to resolve `AccountId`.
- **Opportunity**: Join with `account` to resolve `AccountId` using `account_name` = `account.name`.
- **Project__c**: Join with `account` and `opportunity` to resolve `Account__c` and `Opportunity__c`.
- **Installed_Asset__c**: Join with `account` (handling both `id` and `name` cases) and `project` to resolve `Account__c` and `Project__c`.

Now, I'll start writing the dbt models and `sources.yml`.

---

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='### Step 2: Write the **Account** model. This is straightforward as it maps directly from the source `account` table. The `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` should be populated with the source `id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`). Defaults for `Website`, `BillingCity`, `BillingCountry`, `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `IsDeleted` will be set to `NULL` or sensible defaults.'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Account.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nSELECT\\n    id AS \\"Id\\",\\n    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(name), \'\'), \'Unknown\') AS \\"Name\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'platinum\' THEN \'Platinum\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'gold\' THEN \'Gold\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'silver\' THEN \'Silver\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'bronze\' THEN \'Bronze\'\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(region), \'\') AS \\"Region__c\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(industry), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Website\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n    id AS \\"Legacy_Customer_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }}\\n"}', tool_call_id='0WzFlAawz')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=12999, output_tokens=396), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 39, 344087, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 31, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-bf47aee0e2780841', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="### Step 3: Write the **Contact** model. This requires joining with the `account` table to resolve `AccountId`. For `Role__c` and `Preferred_Language__c`, we'll default to `NULL` since the source does not provide this information."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Contact.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nSELECT\\n    c.id AS \\"Id\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN c.full_name IS NOT NULL THEN\\n            TRIM(SPLIT_PART(c.full_name, \' \', 1))\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"FirstName\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN c.full_name IS NOT NULL THEN\\n            TRIM(\\n                SUBSTRING(\\n                    c.full_name,\\n                    POSITION(\' \' IN c.full_name) + 1\\n                )\\n            )\\n        ELSE \'Unknown\'\\n    END AS \\"LastName\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(c.email), \'\') AS \\"Email\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Phone\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Title\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Role__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Preferred_Language__c\\",\\n    a.id AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n    c.id AS \\"Legacy_Contact_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'contact\') }} c\\nLEFT JOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }} a \\n    ON c.account_ref = a.id\\n"}', tool_call_id='GLxt6c9ly')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=13409, output_tokens=402), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 20, 996020, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 50, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-bd0882b8b6a6a4a0', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

response: ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='### Step 6: Write the **Installed_Asset__c** model. This is the most complex due to the inconsistent `asset.client` field. We need to: 1. First try to join `asset.client` with `account.id`. 2. If that fails, try to join `asset.client` with `account.name`. 3. Join `asset.project` with `project.id` to resolve `Project__c`. The `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` is populated with `asset.id`. The `Warranty_End_Date__c` is populated with `asset.warranty`.'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Installed_Asset__c.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH asset_with_account AS (\\n    SELECT \\n        a.*,\\n        COALESCE(\\n            acc1.id,\\n            acc2.id\\n        ) AS account_id\\n    FROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'asset\') }} a\\n    LEFT JOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }} acc1 \\n        ON a.client = acc1.id\\n    LEFT JOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }} acc2 \\n        ON TRIM(a.client) = TRIM(acc2.name)\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n    a.id AS \\"Id\\",\\n    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.name), \'\'), \'Unknown\') AS \\"Name\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(a.serial), \'\') AS \\"Serial_Number__c\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN a.warranty ~ \'^\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2}$\' THEN a.warranty\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"Warranty_End_Date__c\\",\\n    awa.account_id AS \\"Account__c\\",\\n    p.id AS \\"Project__c\\",\\n    a.id AS \\"Legacy_Asset_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM asset_with_account awa\\nJOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'asset\') }} a ON awa.id = a.id\\nLEFT JOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'project\') }} p \\n    ON a.project = p.id\\n"}', tool_call_id='zK6PN5xmh')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=14996, output_tokens=541), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 33, 35, 417332, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 33, 26, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-91a5ee38a2819512', finish_reason='tool_call')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 29, 48, 286200, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_missing_relations_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_missing_relations_v2_src"."account" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.account  (80 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `tier` text NULL
- `region` text NULL
- `industry` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.asset  (320 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `serial` text NULL
- `warranty` text NULL
- `client` text NULL
- `project` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.contact  (152 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `full_name` text NULL
- `email` text NULL
- `account_ref` text NULL
- `company_name` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.opportunity  (221 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `stage` text NULL
- `amount` double precision NULL
- `customer_number` text NULL
- `account_name` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.project  (112 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `status` text NULL
- `go_live` text NULL
- `client_id` text NULL
- `opportunity_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target. Write one model file per target table listed above — all tables are required.

Self-correction is ON: write complete models; the orchestrator will run dbt after you finish and prompt you to fix failures if needed.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 29, 48, 286211, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 29, 48, 286559, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now I have a clear picture of the relationships and data formats. Here's what I've deduced:

### Key Mappings:
1. **Account to Opportunity**:
   - `opportunity.customer_number` (e.g., `KD-1001`) does **not** directly match `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - However, `opportunity.account_name` matches `account.name` (with some typos like `GRb` vs `GbR`).
   - To map `opportunity` to `account`, we must join on **normalized `account_name` = `account.name`**.
   - The `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` should be populated with `opportunity.customer_number`.

2. **Contact to Account**:
   - `contact.account_ref` directly references `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - Some `account_ref` values are `NULL` or invalid (e.g., `ACC-9103`), so we must handle these with `LEFT JOIN`.
   - The `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` should be populated with `contact.id`.

3. **Project to Account/Opportunity**:
   - `project.client_id` directly references `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - `project.opportunity_ref` directly references `opportunity.id` (e.g., `OPP-00021`).
   - The `Legacy_Project_ID__c` should be populated with `project.id`.

4. **Asset to Account/Project**:
   - `asset.client` is **inconsistent**: sometimes it's an `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1005`), sometimes it's an `account.name` (e.g., `Gnatz AG`).
   - To resolve this, we must **first try to join on `account.id`**, and if that fails, join on `account.name`.
   - `asset.project` directly references `project.id`.
   - The `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` should be populated with `asset.id`.

5. **Enum Mappings**:
   - **Account Tier**: `Bronze`, `Silver`, `Gold`, `Platinum` → map directly to `Customer_Tier__c`.
   - **Opportunity Stage**: Map `stage` values to the allowed enum values in `StageName`.
   - **Project Status**: Map `status` values to `Project_Status__c` (e.g., `Completed`, `On Hold`, etc.).
   - **Contact Role**: Not directly available in source, so we'll default to `NULL` or a sensible default (e.g., `End User`).
   - **Preferred Language**: Not directly available in source, so we'll default to `NULL`.

6. **Dates**:
   - `warranty` and `go_live` are in `YYYY-MM-DD` format, so we can directly cast them to text.
   - For `CreatedDate` and `LastModifiedDate`, since they are not in the source, we'll use `NULL` or a default timestamp.

7. **Amounts**:
   - `opportunity.amount` is already a `double precision`, so we can directly use it.

### Transformation Plan:
- **Account**: Directly map from `fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.account`.
- **Contact**: Join with `account` to resolve `AccountId`.
- **Opportunity**: Join with `account` to resolve `AccountId` using `account_name` = `account.name`.
- **Project__c**: Join with `account` and `opportunity` to resolve `Account__c` and `Opportunity__c`.
- **Installed_Asset__c**: Join with `account` (handling both `id` and `name` cases) and `project` to resolve `Account__c` and `Project__c`.

Now, I'll start writing the dbt models and `sources.yml`.

---

### Step 1: Write `sources.yml`"), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/sources.yml", "content": "version: 2\n\nsources:\n  - name: fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\n    schema: \"fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\"\n    tables:\n      - name: account\n      - name: contact\n      - name: opportunity\n      - name: project\n      - name: asset\n"}', tool_call_id='WiDXPk5OS')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=12040, output_tokens=967), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 21, 411385, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-8a54b3327c4f4d26', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='### Step 2: Write the **Account** model. This is straightforward as it maps directly from the source `account` table. The `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` should be populated with the source `id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`). Defaults for `Website`, `BillingCity`, `BillingCountry`, `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `IsDeleted` will be set to `NULL` or sensible defaults.'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Account.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nSELECT\\n    id AS \\"Id\\",\\n    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(name), \'\'), \'Unknown\') AS \\"Name\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'platinum\' THEN \'Platinum\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'gold\' THEN \'Gold\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'silver\' THEN \'Silver\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'bronze\' THEN \'Bronze\'\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(region), \'\') AS \\"Region__c\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(industry), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Website\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n    id AS \\"Legacy_Customer_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }}\\n"}', tool_call_id='0WzFlAawz')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=12999, output_tokens=396), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 39, 344087, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 31, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-bf47aee0e2780841', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="### Step 3: Write the **Contact** model. This requires joining with the `account` table to resolve `AccountId`. The `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` is populated with `contact.id`. We also need to split `full_name` into `FirstName` and `LastName`. For `Role__c` and `Preferred_Language__c`, we'll default to `NULL` since the source does not provide this information."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Contact.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nSELECT\\n    c.id AS \\"Id\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN c.full_name IS NOT NULL THEN\\n            TRIM(SPLIT_PART(c.full_name, \' \', 1))\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"FirstName\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN c.full_name IS NOT NULL THEN\\n            TRIM(\\n                SUBSTRING(\\n                    c.full_name,\\n                    POSITION(\' \' IN c.full_name) + 1\\n                )\\n            )\\n        ELSE \'Unknown\'\\n    END AS \\"LastName\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(c.email), \'\') AS \\"Email\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Phone\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Title\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Role__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Preferred_Language__c\\",\\n    a.id AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n    c.id AS \\"Legacy_Contact_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'contact\') }} c\\nLEFT JOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }} a \\n    ON c.account_ref = a.id\\n"}', tool_call_id='GLxt6c9ly')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=13409, output_tokens=402), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 20, 996020, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 50, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-bd0882b8b6a6a4a0', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="### Step 4: Write the **Opportunity** model. This requires joining with the `account` table to resolve `AccountId`. Decision Makers\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'perception analysis\' THEN \'Perception Analysis\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) IN (\'proposal\', \'proposal/price quote\') THEN \'Proposal/Price Quote\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) IN (\'negotiation\', \'negotiation/review\') THEN \'Negotiation/Review\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'closed won\' THEN \'Closed Won\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'closed lost\' THEN \'Closed Lost\'\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"StageName\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CloseDate\\",\\n    o.amount AS \\"Amount\\",\\n    \'EUR\' AS \\"CurrencyIsoCode\\",\\n    a.id AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n    o.customer_number AS \\"Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'opportunity\') }} o\\nLEFT JOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }} a \\n    ON TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(LOWER(o.account_name), \'[^a-z0-9\\\\s&\\\\.\\\\-]\', \'\', \'g\')) = \\n       TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(LOWER(a.name), \'[^a-z0-9\\\\s&\\\\.\\\\-]\', \'\', \'g\'))\\n"}', tool_call_id='Y6tmcihfR')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=13825, output_tokens=642), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 47, 464495, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 31, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-ab017a3f929d2032', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM asset_with_account awa\\nJOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'asset\') }} a ON awa.id = a.id\\nLEFT JOIN {{ ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 29, 48, 286200, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_missing_relations_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_missing_relations_v2_src"."account" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.account  (80 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `tier` text NULL
- `region` text NULL
- `industry` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.asset  (320 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `serial` text NULL
- `warranty` text NULL
- `client` text NULL
- `project` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.contact  (152 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `full_name` text NULL
- `email` text NULL
- `account_ref` text NULL
- `company_name` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.opportunity  (221 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `stage` text NULL
- `amount` double precision NULL
- `customer_number` text NULL
- `account_name` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.project  (112 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `status` text NULL
- `go_live` text NULL
- `client_id` text NULL
- `opportunity_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target. Write one model file per target table listed above — all tables are required.

Self-correction is ON: write complete models; the orchestrator will run dbt after you finish and prompt you to fix failures if needed.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 29, 48, 286211, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 29, 48, 286559, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="I need to inspect more data to understand the relationships and formats, especially for:

### Key Mappings:
1. **Account to Opportunity**:
   - `opportunity.customer_number` (e.g., `KD-1001`) does **not** directly match `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).

   - To map `opportunity` to `account`, we must join on **normalized `account_name` = `account.name`**.
   - The `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` should be populated with `opportunity.customer_number`.

2. **Contact to Account**:
   - `contact.account_ref` directly references `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - Some `account_ref` values are `NULL` or invalid (e.g., `ACC-9103`), so we must handle these with `LEFT JOIN`.
   - The `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` should be populated with `contact.id`.

3. **Project to Account/Opportunity**:
   - `project.client_id` directly references `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - `project.opportunity_ref` directly references `opportunity.id` (e.g., `OPP-00021`).
   - The `Legacy_Project_ID__c` should be populated with `project.id`.

4. **Asset to Account/Project**:
   - `asset.client` is **inconsistent**: sometimes it's an `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1005`), sometimes it's an `account.name` (e.g., `Gnatz AG`).
   - To resolve this, we must **first try to join on `account.id`**, and if that fails, join on `account.name`.
   - `asset.project` directly references `project.id`.
   - The `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` should be populated with `asset.id`.

5. **Enum Mappings**:
   - **Account Tier**: `Bronze`, `Silver`, `Gold`, `Platinum` → map directly to `Customer_Tier__c`.
   - **Opportunity Stage**: Map `stage` values to the allowed enum values in `StageName`.
   - **Project Status**: Map `status` values to `Project_Status__c` (e.g., `Completed`, `On Hold`, etc.).
   - **Contact Role**: Not directly available in source, so we'll default to `NULL` or a sensible default (e.g., `End User`).
   - **Preferred Language**: Not directly available in source, so we'll default to `NULL`.

6. **Dates**:
   - `warranty` and `go_live` are in `YYYY-MM-DD` format, so we can directly cast them to text.
   - For `CreatedDate` and `LastModifiedDate`, since they are not in the source, we'll use `NULL` or a default timestamp.

7. **Amounts**:
   - `opportunity.amount` is already a `double precision`, so we can directly use it.

### Transformation Plan:
- **Account**: Directly map from `fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.account`.
- **Contact**: Join with `account` to resolve `AccountId`.
- **Opportunity**: Join with `account` to resolve `AccountId` using `account_name` = `account.name`.
- **Project__c**: Join with `account` and `opportunity` to resolve `Account__c` and `Opportunity__c`.
- **Installed_Asset__c**: Join with `account` (handling both `id` and `name` cases) and `project` to resolve `Account__c` and `Project__c`.

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="### Step 3: Write the **Contact** model. This requires joining with the `account` table to resolve `AccountId`. ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="### Step 4: Write the **Opportunity** model. This requires joining with the `account` table to resolve `AccountId`. Decision Makers\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'perception analysis\' THEN \'Perception Analysis\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) IN (\'proposal\', \'proposal/price quote\') THEN \'Proposal/Price Quote\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) IN (\'negotiation\', \'negotiation/review\') THEN \'Negotiation/Review\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'closed won\' THEN \'Closed Won\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.stage)) = \'closed lost\' THEN \'Closed Lost\'\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"StageName\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CloseDate\\",\\n    o.amount AS \\"Amount\\",\\n    \'EUR\' AS \\"CurrencyIsoCode\\",\\n    a.id AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n    o.customer_number AS \\"Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'opportunity\') }} o\\nLEFT JOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }} a \\n    ON TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(LOWER(o.account_name), \'[^a-z0-9\\\\s&\\\\.\\\\-]\', \'\', \'g\')) = \\n       TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(LOWER(a.name), \'[^a-z0-9\\\\s&\\\\.\\\\-]\', \'\', \'g\'))\\n"}', tool_call_id='Y6tmcihfR')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=13825, output_tokens=642), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 47, 464495, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 31, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-ab017a3f929d2032', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM asset_with_account awa\\nJOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'asset\') }} a ON awa.id = a.id\\nLEFT JOIN {{ ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 29, 48, 286200, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_missing_relations_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_missing_relations_v2_src"."account" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.account  (80 rows)

Columns:
- `id` text NOT NULL
- `name` text NULL
- `tier` text NULL
- `region` text NULL
- `industry` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:

- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target. Write one model file per target table listed above — all tables are required.

Self-correction is ON: write complete models; the orchestrator will run dbt after you finish and prompt you to fix failures if needed.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 29, 48, 286211, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 29, 48, 286559, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now I have a clear picture of the relationships and data formats. Here's what I've deduced:

### Key Mappings:
1. **Account to Opportunity**:
   - `opportunity.customer_number` (e.g., `KD-1001`) does **not** directly match `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - However, `opportunity.account_name` matches `account.name` (with some typos like `GRb` vs `GbR`).
   - To map `opportunity` to `account`, we must join on **normalized `account_name` = `account.name`**.
   - The `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` should be populated with `opportunity.customer_number`.

2. **Contact to Account**:
   - `contact.account_ref` directly references `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - Some `account_ref` values are `NULL` or invalid (e.g., `ACC-9103`), so we must handle these with `LEFT JOIN`.
   - The `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` should be populated with `contact.id`.

3. **Project to Account/Opportunity**:
   - `project.client_id` directly references `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1001`).
   - `project.opportunity_ref` directly references `opportunity.id` (e.g., `OPP-00021`).
   - The `Legacy_Project_ID__c` should be populated with `project.id`.

4. **Asset to Account/Project**:
   - `asset.client` is **inconsistent**: sometimes it's an `account.id` (e.g., `ACC-1005`), sometimes it's an `account.name` (e.g., `Gnatz AG`).
   - To resolve this, we must **first try to join on `account.id`**, and if that fails, join on `account.name`.
   - `asset.project` directly references `project.id`.
   - The `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` should be populated with `asset.id`.

5. **Enum Mappings**:
   - **Account Tier**: `Bronze`, `Silver`, `Gold`, `Platinum` → map directly to `Customer_Tier__c`.
   - **Opportunity Stage**: Map `stage` values to the allowed enum values in `StageName`.
   - **Project Status**: Map `status` values to `Project_Status__c` (e.g., `Completed`, `On Hold`, etc.).
   - **Contact Role**: Not directly available in source, so we'll default to `NULL` or a sensible default (e.g., `End User`).
   - **Preferred Language**: Not directly available in source, so we'll default to `NULL`.

6. **Dates**:
   - `warranty` and `go_live` are in `YYYY-MM-DD` format, so we can directly cast them to text.
   - For `CreatedDate` and `LastModifiedDate`, since they are not in the source, we'll use `NULL` or a default timestamp.

7. **Amounts**:
   - `opportunity.amount` is already a `double precision`, so we can directly use it.

### Transformation Plan:
- **Account**: Directly map from `fixture_missing_relations_v2_src.account`.
- **Contact**: Join with `account` to resolve `AccountId`.
- **Opportunity**: Join with `account` to resolve `AccountId` using `account_name` = `account.name`.
- **Project__c**: Join with `account` and `opportunity` to resolve `Account__c` and `Opportunity__c`.
- **Installed_Asset__c**: Join with `account` (handling both `id` and `name` cases) and `project` to resolve `Account__c` and `Project__c`.

Now, I'll start writing the dbt models and `sources.yml`.

---

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='### Step 2: Write the **Account** model. This is straightforward as it maps directly from the source `account` table. Defaults for `Website`, `BillingCity`, `BillingCountry`, `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `IsDeleted` will be set to `NULL` or sensible defaults.'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Account.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nSELECT\\n    id AS \\"Id\\",\\n    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(name), \'\'), \'Unknown\') AS \\"Name\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'platinum\' THEN \'Platinum\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'gold\' THEN \'Gold\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'silver\' THEN \'Silver\'\\n        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(tier)) = \'bronze\' THEN \'Bronze\'\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(region), \'\') AS \\"Region__c\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(industry), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Website\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n    id AS \\"Legacy_Customer_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }}\\n"}', tool_call_id='0WzFlAawz')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=12999, output_tokens=396), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 39, 344087, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 31, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-bf47aee0e2780841', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="### Step 3: Write the **Contact** model. This requires joining with the `account` table to resolve `AccountId`. For `Role__c` and `Preferred_Language__c`, we'll default to `NULL` since the source does not provide this information."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Contact.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nSELECT\\n    c.id AS \\"Id\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN c.full_name IS NOT NULL THEN\\n            TRIM(SPLIT_PART(c.full_name, \' \', 1))\\n        ELSE NULL\\n    END AS \\"FirstName\\",\\n    CASE \\n        WHEN c.full_name IS NOT NULL THEN\\n            TRIM(\\n                SUBSTRING(\\n                    c.full_name,\\n                    POSITION(\' \' IN c.full_name) + 1\\n                )\\n            )\\n        ELSE \'Unknown\'\\n    END AS \\"LastName\\",\\n    NULLIF(TRIM(c.email), \'\') AS \\"Email\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Phone\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Title\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Role__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"Preferred_Language__c\\",\\n    a.id AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n    c.id AS \\"Legacy_Contact_ID__c\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n    NULL AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n    0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\nFROM {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'contact\') }} c\\nLEFT JOIN {{ source(\'fixture_missing_relations_v2_src\', \'account\') }} a \\n    ON c.account_ref = a.id\\n"}', tool_call_id='GLxt6c9ly')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=13409, output_tokens=402), model_name='nvidia/mistral-medium-3.5-128b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 32, 20, 996020, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 14, 5, 31, 50, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-bd0882b8b6a6a4a0', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d920c156f92', conversation_id='019f5f1a-51fc-7068-b7f9-3d911541821b')
